name: YruRU
description: YruRU is a modern CP-first method created by Yash Mehta in 2020. It solves corner permutation and a line on the bottom (CP Line), then expands into a 1x2x3 block, orients edges while building the right block, and finishes with a reduced last layer. The method is designed to be particularly ergonomic for one-handed (OH) solving due to its RU-dominant moveset. It shares philosophical roots with other CP-first approaches but offers a unique step sequence.

steps.name: CP Line
steps.description: Solve corner permutation for all 8 corners while simultaneously placing two bottom-layer edges to form a line (similar to ZZ's EOLine but for CP). This ensures corners only need orientation later.
tips: Learn to recognize corner permutation states quickly
Plan the full CP Line during inspection

steps.name: Expand to 1x2x3
steps.description: Extend the line into a full 1x2x3 block on the left side by adding two corners and an edge. This creates the foundation for the rest of the solve.
tips: Look ahead during CP Line to plan this step
Minimize rotations by using wide moves
Block should be on the left to enable RU solving

steps.name: EO + Right Block
steps.description: Orient all remaining edges while simultaneously building the right-side 1x2x3 block. Since CP is already solved, this step focuses on EO and blockbuilding using primarily R and U moves.
tips: Combine EO with pair insertion when possible
RU moveset is very ergonomic, especially for OH
Track edge orientation during left block construction

steps.name: Reduced Last Layer
steps.description: Solve the last layer with a reduced set of algorithms. Corner permutation and edge orientation are already resolved, so only corner orientation and edge permutation remain. This can be done with 2GLL, COLL+EPLL, or other reduced sets.
tips: Only ~30 cases with CP and EO pre-solved
COLL + EPLL is the standard approach
Recognition is much simpler than full OLL+PLL

pros: Reduced last layer due to CP-first approach
cons: CP recognition has a steep learning curve
Planning CP Line in inspection is challenging
Very new method with limited proven results
Requires understanding of corner permutation theory
creator: Yash Mehta
